rawText:
China's domestic priorities are shifting in ways that reveal the tensions at the heart of its development model. The country's once-dramatic progress on air quality has slowed sharply, public attitudes toward the United States and Taiwan have hardened, and the government is pivoting to exploit a massive energy advantage in the global race for artificial intelligence dominance.

Between 2013 and 2021, China's population-weighted levels of fine particulate matter fell from sixty-six micrograms per cubic meter to thirty-three, a pace of roughly four units per year. Since then, the decline has nearly stalled, dropping just half a unit annually. The initial gains came from relatively straightforward measures like installing scrubbers on power plants, but deeper reductions now require structural changes to transport and industry. Climate change is compounding the challenge: more frequent sandstorms and atmospheric inversions trap pollution at ground level. The government recently announced it will lower the national limit for fine particulates from thirty-five to twenty-five micrograms, still five times the guideline recommended by the World Health Organization, and officials acknowledge that moving too aggressively risks slowing an already fragile economy.

Meanwhile, public sentiment is taking a harder line on foreign affairs. A rare series of opinion polls conducted across 2024 and 2025, surveying sixty-five hundred people, found that support for using military force to unify China and Taiwan rose from a quarter to nearly half. Almost three-quarters of respondents now view the United States as a national security threat, and sixty-two percent favor retaliatory measures against American trade restrictions even at economic cost to China. High-income respondents tend to view American culture more favorably yet are also more hawkish about projecting Chinese power, a contradiction the government has previously tried to suppress in polling data.

Beneath both of these shifts lies a strategic bet on energy. China's power grid, already the world's largest at thirty-eight hundred gigawatts, added more than five hundred gigawatts of capacity last year alone. Data centers in China secure electricity at roughly three cents per kilowatt-hour, about half the rate many American facilities pay. While American chip export controls have limited Chinese spending on AI infrastructure to a fraction of its rival's, domestic chip production is expanding rapidly, and the latest five-year plan calls for exploring development paths toward artificial general intelligence for the first time.

The connecting thread is a country recalibrating the balance between growth and restraint across multiple domains. On pollution, the easy gains are spent. On foreign policy, the public appetite for accommodation is shrinking. And on technology, the strategy is to convert a surplus in one resource — cheap electricity — into leverage in the contest that may define the next decade.

overview.title: China's Shifting Calculus: Pollution, Power, and Public Opinion

sentences.original: China's domestic priorities are shifting in ways that reveal the tensions at the heart of its development model.
sentences.translation: 중국의 국내 우선순위가 발전 모델의 핵심에 놓인 긴장을 드러내는 방식으로 변하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: in ways that reveal the tensions at the heart of its development model
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + that 관계절

vocabulary.example: Governments must constantly reassess their domestic priorities.

expressions.usage: 어떤 문제나 체계의 가장 근본적인 부분을 가리킬 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s [domain] priorities are shifting in ways that reveal the tensions at the heart of [system]
patterns.explanation: 변화가 단순한 조정이 아니라 근본적 모순을 드러냄을 제시하는 도입 패턴
examples: Europe's energy priorities are shifting in ways that reveal the tensions at the heart of its climate agenda.
America's trade priorities are shifting in ways that reveal the tensions at the heart of its alliance system.

drillQuestions.question: China's domestic priorities are shifting in ways that reveal the tensions at the ______ of its development model.

sentences.notes: 도입 문장으로, '긴장'이라는 단어가 글 전체의 모순적 주제들을 예고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: China's domestic priorities
are shifting in ways that reveal
the tensions at the heart of
its development model

sentences.original: The country's once-dramatic progress on air quality has slowed sharply, public attitudes toward the United States and Taiwan have hardened, and the government is pivoting to exploit a massive energy advantage in the global race for artificial intelligence dominance.
sentences.translation: 한때 극적이었던 대기질 개선이 급격히 둔화되었고, 미국과 대만에 대한 여론은 강경해졌으며, 정부는 인공지능 패권을 위한 글로벌 경쟁에서 막대한 에너지 우위를 활용하는 쪽으로 방향을 전환하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The country's once-dramatic progress on air quality
grammarElements.label: 주어 1

grammarElements.text: public attitudes toward the United States and Taiwan
grammarElements.label: 주어 2

grammarElements.text: and the government is pivoting to exploit a massive energy advantage
grammarElements.label: 등위절 3

vocabulary.example: The company is pivoting from hardware to software services.
The race for AI dominance is reshaping global alliances.

expressions.usage: 전략적 방향 전환과 그 목적을 한 구절로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Progress A] has slowed, [attitude B] has hardened, and [actor C] is pivoting to [action]
patterns.explanation: 세 가지 동시 변화를 병렬로 요약하는 패턴
examples: Economic growth has slowed, public patience has hardened, and the opposition is pivoting to exploit voter frustration.
Diplomatic progress has slowed, rhetoric has hardened, and both sides are pivoting to prepare for confrontation.

drillQuestions.question: The government is ______ to exploit a massive energy advantage in the global race for AI dominance.

sentences.notes: 3중 병렬 구조(has slowed / have hardened / is pivoting)가 글의 세 가지 주제를 미리 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: once-dramatic progress on air quality has slowed sharply
koreanAlignment.ko: 한때 극적이었던 대기질 개선이 급격히 둔화되었다

koreanAlignment.en: public attitudes have hardened
the government is pivoting to exploit
koreanAlignment.ko: 정부는 활용하는 쪽으로 방향을 전환하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: a massive energy advantage in the global race for AI dominance
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 패권을 위한 글로벌 경쟁에서의 막대한 에너지 우위

sentences.original: Between 2013 and 2021, China's population-weighted levels of fine particulate matter fell from sixty-six micrograms per cubic meter to thirty-three, a pace of roughly four units per year.
sentences.translation: 2013년에서 2021년 사이에 중국의 인구 가중 미세먼지 농도는 입방미터당 66마이크로그램에서 33으로 떨어졌으며, 이는 연간 약 4단위의 속도였다.

grammarElements.text: China's population-weighted levels of fine particulate matter
grammarElements.label: 주어 (복합 명사구)

grammarElements.text: from sixty-six micrograms per cubic meter to thirty-three
grammarElements.label: from A to B 보어

grammarElements.text: a pace of roughly four units per year
grammarElements.label: 동격어구 (보충 설명)

vocabulary.example: Fine particulate matter is one of the most harmful forms of air pollution.
Population-weighted averages give more importance to readings in densely populated areas.

expressions.usage: 수치적 변화의 속도를 보충 설명할 때 동격으로 사용

patterns.pattern: Between [year] and [year], [metric] fell from [A] to [B], a pace of roughly [rate]
patterns.explanation: 시간 범위, 수치 변화, 속도를 한 문장에 담는 데이터 제시 패턴
examples: Between 2010 and 2020, the poverty rate fell from fifteen percent to eight, a pace of roughly seven-tenths of a point per year.
Between 2015 and 2023, the cost of solar panels fell from sixty cents per watt to twenty, a pace of roughly five cents per year.

drillQuestions.question: China's levels of fine particulate ______ fell from sixty-six to thirty-three micrograms.

sentences.notes: 구체적 수치(66→33, 연 4단위)가 뒤에 나올 '둔화'와 대비되기 위해 먼저 제시된다.

koreanAlignment.en: Between 2013 and 2021
population-weighted levels of fine particulate matter
fell from sixty-six to thirty-three
a pace of roughly four units per year

sentences.original: Since then, the decline has nearly stalled, dropping just half a unit annually.
sentences.translation: 그 이후로 감소세는 거의 멈추다시피 하여 연간 겨우 0.5단위만 떨어지고 있다.

grammarElements.text: dropping just half a unit annually
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (구체화)

vocabulary.example: Negotiations have stalled over disagreements about trade tariffs.

expressions.usage: 이전에 언급한 시점을 기준으로 이후 변화를 서술할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Since then, [trend] has nearly stalled, [verb-ing] just [small amount]
patterns.explanation: 이전의 빠른 변화와 대비하여 급격한 둔화를 강조하는 패턴
examples: Since then, wage growth has nearly stalled, rising just one percent annually.
Since then, the reform effort has nearly stalled, producing just two bills in three years.

drillQuestions.question: Since then, the decline has nearly ______, dropping just half a unit annually.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 '연 4단위'와 이 문장의 '겨우 0.5단위'의 대비가 둔화의 심각성을 극적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Since then
the decline has nearly stalled
dropping just half a unit annually

sentences.original: The initial gains came from relatively straightforward measures like installing scrubbers on power plants, but deeper reductions now require structural changes to transport and industry.
sentences.translation: 초기 성과는 발전소에 세정기를 설치하는 것과 같은 비교적 단순한 조치에서 나왔지만, 더 깊은 감소를 위해서는 이제 운송과 산업에 대한 구조적 변화가 필요하다.

grammarElements.text: relatively straightforward measures like installing scrubbers on power plants
grammarElements.label: 전치사 보어 + like 예시

grammarElements.text: but deeper reductions now require structural changes to transport and industry
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: Installing scrubbers on smokestacks can remove up to ninety percent of sulfur emissions.
The initial phase of reform was relatively straightforward.

expressions.usage: 개혁이나 정책의 쉬운 초기 성과를 가리킬 때 사용, 이후 어려움과 대비됨

patterns.pattern: The initial gains came from [easy measures], but deeper [progress] now requires [harder changes]
patterns.explanation: '쉬운 과일'은 이미 따고, 이제 어려운 과제가 남았음을 대비하는 패턴
examples: The initial gains came from cutting obvious waste, but deeper savings now require rethinking the entire supply chain.
The initial gains came from digitising paper records, but deeper transformation now requires rebuilding core systems.

drillQuestions.question: Deeper reductions now require structural ______ to transport and industry.

sentences.notes: initial과 deeper의 대비가 '쉬운 개선은 끝나고 어려운 단계에 접어들었다'는 핵심 논점을 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The initial gains came from relatively straightforward measures
koreanAlignment.ko: 초기 성과는 비교적 단순한 조치에서 나왔다

koreanAlignment.en: like installing scrubbers on power plants
deeper reductions now require
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 깊은 감소를 위해서는 이제 필요하다

koreanAlignment.en: structural changes to transport and industry

sentences.original: Climate change is compounding the challenge: more frequent sandstorms and atmospheric inversions trap pollution at ground level.
sentences.translation: 기후 변화가 도전을 가중시키고 있다: 더 빈번한 모래폭풍과 대기 역전 현상이 오염 물질을 지표면에 가둔다.

grammarElements.text: more frequent sandstorms and atmospheric inversions trap pollution at ground level
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절

vocabulary.example: Rising interest rates are compounding the pressure on indebted households.
Atmospheric inversions in winter create smog-trapping conditions over cities.

expressions.usage: 오염 물질이 높이 퍼지지 못하고 사람이 숨 쉬는 높이에 머무름을 나타냄

patterns.explanation: 콜론으로 일반 진술 뒤에 구체적 메커니즘을 설명하는 패턴
examples: Aging infrastructure is compounding the challenge: older pipes leak twice as much water as new ones.
Remote work is compounding the challenge: managers struggle to assess performance without in-person observation.

drillQuestions.question: Climate change is ______ the challenge of reducing air pollution.

sentences.notes: 콜론이 '어떻게 가중시키는가'를 즉시 구체화하여 독자의 이해를 돕는다.

koreanAlignment.en: Climate change is compounding the challenge
more frequent sandstorms
atmospheric inversions
trap pollution at ground level

sentences.original: The government recently announced it will lower the national limit for fine particulates from thirty-five to twenty-five micrograms, still five times the guideline recommended by the World Health Organization, and officials acknowledge that moving too aggressively risks slowing an already fragile economy.
sentences.translation: 정부는 최근 미세먼지 국가 기준을 35마이크로그램에서 25마이크로그램으로 낮추겠다고 발표했는데, 이는 여전히 세계보건기구 권고 기준의 5배이며, 관리들은 너무 공격적으로 움직이면 이미 취약한 경제를 둔화시킬 위험이 있음을 인정한다.

grammarElements.text: it will lower the national limit for fine particulates from thirty-five to twenty-five micrograms
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어 (that 생략)

grammarElements.text: still five times the guideline recommended by the World Health Organization
grammarElements.label: 동격 수식어 (대비)

grammarElements.text: and officials acknowledge that moving too aggressively risks slowing an already fragile economy
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: Moving too aggressively on regulation can stifle innovation.
The post-pandemic recovery remains fragile in many developing economies.

expressions.usage: risk + 동명사 구문으로 부정적 결과의 가능성을 나타냄

patterns.pattern: [Authority] announced it will [reform], still [gap from ideal], and officials acknowledge that [trade-off]
patterns.explanation: 정책 발표, 이상과의 격차, 관리들의 솔직한 인정을 한 문장에 담는 패턴
examples: The ministry announced it will raise the minimum wage to fifteen dollars, still below the living wage in most cities, and officials acknowledge that moving faster risks triggering layoffs.
The central bank announced it will cut rates by a quarter point, still above the level businesses need, and officials acknowledge that deeper cuts risk fuelling inflation.

drillQuestions.question: The new limit is still ______ times the guideline recommended by the WHO.
Officials acknowledge that moving too aggressively risks ______ an already fragile economy.

sentences.notes: still five times가 개선에도 불구하고 여전히 막대한 격차가 있음을 짧고 강렬하게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The government recently announced it will lower the national limit
koreanAlignment.ko: 정부는 최근 국가 기준을 낮추겠다고 발표했다

koreanAlignment.en: still five times the guideline recommended by the WHO
officials acknowledge that
moving too aggressively risks slowing an already fragile economy
koreanAlignment.ko: 너무 공격적으로 움직이면 이미 취약한 경제를 둔화시킬 위험이 있다

sentences.original: Meanwhile, public sentiment is taking a harder line on foreign affairs.
sentences.translation: 한편, 여론은 외교 문제에 대해 더 강경한 노선을 취하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: a harder line on foreign affairs
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: Public sentiment has shifted dramatically against the trade deal.

expressions.usage: 입장이 더 엄격하거나 타협 불가능해졌음을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: Meanwhile, [subject] is taking a harder line on [issue]
patterns.explanation: 문단 전환 시 새 주제의 경직화를 소개하는 패턴
examples: Meanwhile, regulators are taking a harder line on Big Tech.
Meanwhile, voters are taking a harder line on immigration.

drillQuestions.question: Public sentiment is taking a harder ______ on foreign affairs.

sentences.notes: Meanwhile가 대기질에서 외교 여론으로 주제를 전환하는 표지 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Meanwhile
public sentiment is taking a harder line
koreanAlignment.ko: 여론은 더 강경한 노선을 취하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: on foreign affairs

sentences.original: A rare series of opinion polls conducted across 2024 and 2025, surveying sixty-five hundred people, found that support for using military force to unify China and Taiwan rose from a quarter to nearly half.
sentences.translation: 2024년과 2025년에 걸쳐 6,500명을 대상으로 실시된 드문 연속 여론조사에서, 중국과 대만을 통일하기 위해 무력을 사용하는 것에 대한 지지가 4분의 1에서 거의 절반으로 상승한 것으로 나타났다.

grammarElements.text: A rare series of opinion polls conducted across 2024 and 2025
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 과거분사 수식어

grammarElements.text: that support for using military force to unify China and Taiwan rose from a quarter to nearly half
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어

vocabulary.example: Calls to unify the two territories by force have grown louder.
Researchers surveying thousands of respondents found significant regional differences.

expressions.usage: 중국처럼 여론조사가 제한된 환경에서 데이터의 희소성을 강조

patterns.pattern: A [rare/landmark] series of [studies], surveying [sample], found that [finding with from-to change]
patterns.explanation: 연구의 규모와 희소성을 강조하면서 핵심 결과를 전달하는 패턴
examples: A landmark series of clinical trials, enrolling twelve thousand patients, found that the drug reduced mortality from eight percent to three.
A rare series of surveys, interviewing workers across ten countries, found that trust in management fell from sixty percent to thirty-five.

drillQuestions.question: Support for using military force rose from a quarter to nearly ______.

sentences.notes: from a quarter to nearly half라는 배로 뛴 수치가 여론 경직화의 규모를 명확히 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A rare series of opinion polls
surveying sixty-five hundred people
support for using military force to unify China and Taiwan
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국과 대만을 통일하기 위해 무력을 사용하는 것에 대한 지지

koreanAlignment.en: rose from a quarter to nearly half

sentences.original: Almost three-quarters of respondents now view the United States as a national security threat, and sixty-two percent favor retaliatory measures against American trade restrictions even at economic cost to China.
sentences.translation: 응답자의 거의 4분의 3이 현재 미국을 국가안보 위협으로 보고 있으며, 62%는 중국에 경제적 비용이 들더라도 미국의 무역 제한에 대한 보복 조치를 지지한다.

grammarElements.text: the United States as a national security threat
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + as 보어

grammarElements.text: and sixty-two percent favor retaliatory measures against American trade restrictions even at economic cost to China
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The government announced retaliatory tariffs on American agricultural products.
A majority of respondents expressed dissatisfaction with the current policy.

expressions.usage: 대가를 감수하겠다는 의지의 강도를 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Fraction] of respondents now view [entity] as [threat], and [percentage] favor [action] even at [cost]
patterns.explanation: 여론조사 결과를 두 가지 수치로 연속 제시하여 경직화를 보여주는 패턴
examples: Almost two-thirds of voters now view inflation as the top threat, and fifty-eight percent favor interest rate hikes even at the cost of higher unemployment.
Nearly half of citizens now view immigration as a security threat, and forty-five percent favor stricter controls even at economic cost.

drillQuestions.question: Sixty-two percent favor ______ measures against American trade restrictions.

sentences.notes: even at economic cost가 여론의 강경함을 가장 효과적으로 전달하는 핵심 구절이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Almost three-quarters of respondents
view the United States as a national security threat
favor retaliatory measures
even at economic cost to China

pronunciationNotes: retaliatory: /rɪˈtæliətɔːri/ 두 번째 음절에 강세, 다섯 음절

sentences.original: High-income respondents tend to view American culture more favorably yet are also more hawkish about projecting Chinese power, a contradiction the government has previously tried to suppress in polling data.
sentences.translation: 고소득 응답자들은 미국 문화를 더 호의적으로 보는 경향이 있으면서도 중국의 힘을 투사하는 것에 대해서는 더 강경한데, 이는 정부가 이전에 여론조사 데이터에서 억누르려 했던 모순이다.

grammarElements.text: tend to view ... yet are also more hawkish
grammarElements.label: 병렬 동사 (tend to + yet are)

grammarElements.text: a contradiction the government has previously tried to suppress in polling data
grammarElements.label: 동격어구 (보충 설명)

vocabulary.example: The minister adopted a hawkish stance on defence spending.
Authoritarian regimes often suppress polling data that contradicts official narratives.

expressions.usage: 군사적·외교적 영향력을 외부로 행사하는 것을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Group] tend to [positive view] yet are also more [aggressive attitude], a contradiction [authority] has tried to [suppress/manage]
patterns.explanation: 한 집단 내의 모순적 태도와 당국의 대응을 한 문장에 담는 패턴
examples: Young voters tend to support free trade yet are also more protectionist about domestic jobs, a contradiction politicians have struggled to address.
Tech executives tend to advocate privacy yet are also more aggressive about data collection, a contradiction regulators have tried to resolve.

drillQuestions.question: High-income respondents are more ______ about projecting Chinese power.

sentences.notes: yet이 한 집단 안의 모순(미국 문화 호감 + 중국 힘 투사 강경)을 선명하게 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: High-income respondents tend to view American culture more favorably
koreanAlignment.ko: 고소득 응답자들은 미국 문화를 더 호의적으로 보는 경향이 있다

koreanAlignment.en: yet are also more hawkish about projecting Chinese power
koreanAlignment.ko: 중국의 힘을 투사하는 것에 대해서는 더 강경하다

koreanAlignment.en: a contradiction
the government has previously tried to suppress in polling data
koreanAlignment.ko: 정부가 이전에 여론조사 데이터에서 억누르려 했던

sentences.original: Beneath both of these shifts lies a strategic bet on energy.
sentences.translation: 이 두 변화의 이면에는 에너지에 대한 전략적 베팅이 놓여 있다.

vocabulary.example: The merger represents a strategic bet on the future of electric vehicles.

expressions.usage: 표면적 현상 아래에 더 깊은 원인이 있음을 나타내는 도치 표현

patterns.explanation: 도치 구문으로 표면 아래의 핵심 요인을 드라마틱하게 제시하는 패턴
examples: Beneath the trade war rhetoric lies a deeper contest for technological supremacy.
Beneath the budget cuts lies a fundamental rethinking of the role of government.

drillQuestions.question: Beneath both of these shifts ______ a strategic bet on energy.

sentences.notes: 도치 구문(Beneath ... lies ...)이 새 문단의 핵심 주장을 극적으로 도입한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Beneath both of these shifts
lies a strategic bet
on energy

sentences.original: China's power grid, already the world's largest at thirty-eight hundred gigawatts, added more than five hundred gigawatts of capacity last year alone.
sentences.translation: 이미 3,800기가와트로 세계 최대인 중국의 전력망은 지난해에만 500기가와트 이상의 용량을 추가했다.

grammarElements.text: already the world's largest at thirty-eight hundred gigawatts
grammarElements.label: 동격 삽입구

grammarElements.text: more than five hundred gigawatts of capacity
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The country added more renewable capacity last year than any other nation.

expressions.usage: alone을 붙여 단일 연도의 수치가 이미 놀라울 만큼 크다는 점을 강조

patterns.pattern: [System], already the world's largest at [size], added [impressive amount] last year alone
patterns.explanation: 이미 거대한 것이 더 커지고 있음을 동격구와 alone으로 강조하는 패턴
examples: India's railway network, already the world's fourth-largest at sixty-eight thousand kilometres, added three thousand kilometres of track last year alone.
America's prison system, already the world's largest at two million inmates, added fifty thousand beds last year alone.

drillQuestions.question: China's power grid added more than five hundred gigawatts of ______ last year alone.

sentences.notes: already ... at 삽입구가 기존 규모를, last year alone이 추가 확장의 속도를 각각 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: China's power grid
already the world's largest at thirty-eight hundred gigawatts
added more than five hundred gigawatts of capacity
last year alone

sentences.original: Data centers in China secure electricity at roughly three cents per kilowatt-hour, about half the rate many American facilities pay.
sentences.translation: 중국의 데이터 센터는 킬로와트시당 약 3센트에 전기를 확보하는데, 이는 많은 미국 시설이 지불하는 요금의 약 절반이다.

grammarElements.text: electricity at roughly three cents per kilowatt-hour
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 가격 전치사구

grammarElements.text: about half the rate many American facilities pay
grammarElements.label: 동격 비교구

vocabulary.example: Companies that secure cheap energy gain a lasting competitive advantage.

expressions.usage: 두 대상 간 가격 차이를 간결하게 비교할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] secure [resource] at [price], about [fraction] the rate [competitor] pays
patterns.explanation: 자원 가격의 비교우위를 분수로 표현하는 패턴
examples: Manufacturers in Vietnam secure labour at roughly two dollars per hour, about a third the rate Chinese factories pay.
Solar farms in the Middle East secure land at roughly ten cents per square meter, about a quarter the rate European developers pay.

drillQuestions.question: Data centers in China secure electricity at roughly ______ cents per kilowatt-hour.

sentences.notes: about half the rate라는 간결한 비교가 중국의 에너지 비용 우위를 즉시 이해시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: Data centers in China secure electricity
at roughly three cents per kilowatt-hour
about half the rate
many American facilities pay

sentences.original: While American chip export controls have limited Chinese spending on AI infrastructure to a fraction of its rival's, domestic chip production is expanding rapidly, and the latest five-year plan calls for exploring development paths toward artificial general intelligence for the first time.
sentences.translation: 미국의 칩 수출 규제가 중국의 AI 인프라 지출을 경쟁국의 일부로 제한했지만, 국내 칩 생산은 빠르게 확대되고 있으며, 최신 5개년 계획은 처음으로 범용 인공지능을 향한 개발 경로 탐색을 명시하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: While American chip export controls have limited Chinese spending on AI infrastructure to a fraction of its rival's
grammarElements.label: While 양보절

grammarElements.text: and the latest five-year plan calls for exploring development paths toward artificial general intelligence for the first time
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: Chinese AI spending remains a fraction of what American companies invest.
The race toward artificial general intelligence is intensifying among major powers.

expressions.usage: 공식 문서나 정책이 특정 행동을 촉구함을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: While [constraint] has limited [spending] to a fraction of [rival's], [domestic response] is expanding rapidly, and [official plan] calls for [ambitious goal] for the first time
patterns.explanation: 외부 제약에도 불구하고 내부 대응이 가속되고 있음을 보여주는 3절 패턴
examples: While sanctions have limited Russian access to Western technology, domestic production is expanding rapidly, and the latest military plan calls for full import substitution for the first time.

While tariffs have limited European exports to a fraction of prior levels, local manufacturing is expanding rapidly, and the latest industrial strategy calls for achieving supply-chain independence for the first time.

drillQuestions.question: The latest five-year plan ______ for exploring development paths toward artificial general intelligence.
American chip export controls have limited Chinese spending to a ______ of its rival's.

sentences.notes: While절이 제약을, 주절이 그에도 불구한 확장을, for the first time이 야망의 새 단계를 각각 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: While American chip export controls have limited Chinese spending
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국의 칩 수출 규제가 중국의 지출을 제한했지만

koreanAlignment.en: to a fraction of its rival's
domestic chip production is expanding rapidly
koreanAlignment.ko: 국내 칩 생산은 빠르게 확대되고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the latest five-year plan calls for exploring development paths toward artificial general intelligence for the first time
koreanAlignment.ko: 최신 5개년 계획은 처음으로 범용 인공지능을 향한 개발 경로 탐색을 명시한다

pronunciationNotes: artificial general intelligence: 각 단어 첫 음절에 강세, 약칭 AGI /eɪ dʒiː aɪ/

sentences.original: The connecting thread is a country recalibrating the balance between growth and restraint across multiple domains.
sentences.translation: 이를 관통하는 맥락은 한 나라가 여러 영역에 걸쳐 성장과 자제 사이의 균형을 재조정하고 있다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: a country recalibrating the balance between growth and restraint across multiple domains
grammarElements.label: 보어 (현재분사 수식)

vocabulary.example: Central banks are recalibrating their interest rate strategies.
Fiscal restraint has become essential as debt levels rise.

expressions.usage: 여러 주제를 하나의 핵심으로 묶을 때 사용하는 결론 표현

patterns.pattern: The connecting thread is [subject] recalibrating the balance between [tension A] and [tension B] across [scope]
patterns.explanation: 여러 사례를 '균형 재조정'이라는 하나의 주제로 종합하는 결론 패턴
examples: The connecting thread is Europe recalibrating the balance between solidarity and sovereignty across economic policy.
The connecting thread is the tech industry recalibrating the balance between innovation and regulation across markets.

drillQuestions.question: The connecting thread is a country ______ the balance between growth and restraint.

sentences.notes: growth와 restraint의 대비가 글의 핵심 긴장을 한 단어 쌍으로 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The connecting thread is
a country recalibrating the balance
between growth and restraint
across multiple domains

sentences.original: On pollution, the easy gains are spent.

vocabulary.example: After a decade of rapid reform, the easy gains are spent.

expressions.usage: 저매달린 과일은 이미 다 땄고 이제 어려운 과제만 남았음을 나타냄

patterns.pattern: On [domain], the easy gains are spent
patterns.explanation: 짧은 단정문으로 한 영역의 상태를 선언하는 결론 패턴
examples: On education reform, the easy gains are spent.
On cost reduction, the easy gains are spent.

drillQuestions.question: On pollution, the easy gains are ______.

sentences.notes: 극도로 짧은 문장으로, 앞서 긴 문장들로 전개한 대기질 논의를 한마디로 요약한다.

koreanAlignment.en: On pollution
the easy gains are spent

sentences.original: On foreign policy, the public appetite for accommodation is shrinking.
sentences.translation: 외교 정책에서는 타협에 대한 대중의 의욕이 줄어들고 있다.

vocabulary.example: There is little appetite for accommodation on either side of the dispute.

expressions.usage: 양보하려는 의지가 줄어드는 상황을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: On [domain], the [public/political] appetite for [concession] is shrinking
patterns.explanation: 앞 문장과 병렬 구조를 이루며 다른 영역의 상태를 선언하는 패턴
examples: On trade, the political appetite for compromise is shrinking.
On immigration, the public appetite for openness is shrinking.

drillQuestions.question: The public appetite for ______ is shrinking on foreign policy.

sentences.notes: accommodation이 단순한 '숙소'가 아닌 '타협, 양보'라는 외교적 의미로 쓰인 점에 주의.

koreanAlignment.en: On foreign policy
the public appetite for accommodation
is shrinking

pronunciationNotes: accommodation: /əˌkɒməˈdeɪʃən/ 네 번째 음절에 강세, 다섯 음절

sentences.original: And on technology, the strategy is to convert a surplus in one resource — cheap electricity — into leverage in the contest that may define the next decade.
sentences.translation: 그리고 기술에서는 하나의 자원 — 저렴한 전기 — 의 잉여를 향후 10년을 결정지을 수 있는 경쟁에서의 지렛대로 전환하는 것이 전략이다.

grammarElements.text: to convert a surplus in one resource — cheap electricity — into leverage in the contest that may define the next decade
grammarElements.label: to부정사 보어

vocabulary.example: China aims to convert its energy surplus into a strategic advantage.
Cheap energy gives China leverage in the race for AI dominance.

expressions.usage: 한 종류의 자원이나 우위를 다른 영역의 힘으로 바꿈을 표현

patterns.pattern: And on [third domain], the strategy is to convert [advantage A] into [advantage B] in the contest that may define [timeframe]
patterns.explanation: 3단 병렬의 마지막으로, 전략의 핵심을 '전환'으로 요약하는 결론 패턴
examples: And on defence, the strategy is to convert manufacturing scale into military readiness in the contest that may define the next era.
And on education, the strategy is to convert digital access into human capital in the contest that may define the century.

drillQuestions.question: The strategy is to convert a surplus in one resource into ______ in the contest that may define the next decade.
China aims to convert cheap ______ into a competitive advantage.

sentences.notes: em dash(—)로 삽입된 cheap electricity가 추상적 '잉여'를 구체화하며, 글 전체의 결론을 장식한다.

koreanAlignment.en: And on technology, the strategy is to convert
a surplus in one resource — cheap electricity —
koreanAlignment.ko: 하나의 자원 — 저렴한 전기 — 의 잉여를

koreanAlignment.en: into leverage
in the contest that may define the next decade
